matches.moment: Norway concede from a set piece they should have cleared, and England's extra sharpness after Bellingham's 78th-minute strike proves decisive rather than any individual moment of brilliance.
Ndoye springs Argentina's high line on the counter for a late consolation, but it's Messi splitting a deep block early -- not a set piece or error -- that sets the tone.

verdict: Both quarterfinals close a day that, on paper, favors the established order -- England and Argentina -- but the underlying numbers refuse to hand either favorite a comfortable passage. Norway arrives with the tournament's hottest finisher in Haaland and a full-strength defensive record considerably tighter than their rotated France dead-rubber suggests, while England's Quansah suspension and Henderson's tournament-ending injury thin a squad that has needed late drama in three of its last four knockout-adjacent matches. Argentina, despite topping the tournament's xG charts and riding Messi's finest World Cup form, has now conceded in four of its last five matches and trailed by two goals as recently as the round of 16 -- the underlying data says this Argentina side is beatable, even if Switzerland's own attack, further blunted by Manzambi's absence, likely lacks the firepower to be the one that does it. If both favorites advance as projected, Saturday's semifinal pairing sets up England's suspect knockout-round defense against an Argentina side that has proven this tournament it can be hurt -- the kind of matchup that would make the model's cautious instincts today look prescient rather than timid.

players.name: Erling Haaland
players.watch: First real test against a top-tier organized back line this tournament

players.watch: Penalty-box instincts against a defense that concedes chances even when winning
